metadata.name: SoulFish #395
metadata.description:
Named after the artist Wu Che-Yu, the soul fish was born in the void and crystallized from human emotions.

Fluttering silently and slowly, their bodies almost disappeared in the light of the deep sea.
The fish swims freely, and this is the happiness of the fish.
If we are not a fish, how do we know the joy of a fish?
How do we know that we do not know the joy of fish?
We will only learn about it when we come to the riverside.

Inspired by Zhuāngzǐ: "The Debate on the Joy of Fish" c. 3rd century BC

Interactivities:
> Key S: Save
> Holding mouse in different directions: Swim 
> Click and let the fish touch the Bubbles
> Key B: Toggle border
> Key P: Pause

metadata.name: Aeꜩer  #2
metadata.description:
Aether is a universal medium that fills all space, just as air is the medium for sound propagation.

Click on to play embeed audio (10626KB) minimalist drone patch recorded on vcvrack 2.0, opensource modular synthesizer.

Live code generative visuals patch on hydra.ojack.xyz

"Aeꜩer" by Marcelo Moura, dec/2021

visit. deadmarcelo.tez.page

metadata.name: SCRIBBLERS #77
metadata.description:
Generative animated drawing inspired by mindless doodling and low-res CAD graphics. Custom physics engine made with vanilla javascript. Colors quantized with GLSL. By @galoandstuff.

Use mouse/touch to drag scribbles around and untangle them.
Press ’s’ key to save PNG (live mode only).
Screen size responsive.

metadata.name: Orbicular #64
metadata.description:
Generative constellation of planets, a combination of digital and non-digital art.
Created by Pawel Grzelak and Tom Blameworth.

Paint orbs created by Tom, are randomly chosen, placed, rotated, cropped, and shaded.
Various digital elements like cities, planetary rings or background are procedurally generated, and are unique to each edition.


Detailed project breakdown - https://pawelgrzelak.com/orbicular


Press "S" to save 2000px file. This can be slow.

---

Project uses p5.js library.

LICENCE.md included.

metadata.name: Sunlit Sanctuary #32
metadata.description:
[ CLICK TO START THE SOUND ]

Welcome to the Sunlit Sanctuary, where the warm rays of the sun dance along its daily trajectory. As the sun's light illuminates the space, it gently caresses the musical instruments scattered throughout the sanctuary, coaxing them into producing a symphony of harmonious sounds. You can enjoy the pre-made animation or take control of it yourself!

Controls
[ f ] - fullscreen
[ s ] - save the current frame as an image
[ cursor / finger ] - control the position of the sun

Created with Three.js & Tone.js
Copyright (c) March 2023 Shaderism

metadata.name: FERAL #52
metadata.description:
FERAL tries to continue to explore further where PRIMAL left by adding more layers of noise to shape, distort and warp a grid to create topologies...

Enjoy and thanks a million times for being a part of my journey!

metadata.name: Possible Ways #243
metadata.description:
This project is written in javascript without using any other libraries, and it is an endless real-time software animation.

The appearance of each token is initially determined by the hash, but the size and positions of its elements change randomly over time, creating an infinite performance.

While working on this project, I was inspired by the elusiveness of the moment, that cannot be saved or predicted. It can only be experienced.

metadata.name: Manu #222
metadata.description:
Manu
---------------

A collector-led exploration of minimalist imperfect line work.

Outputs are generated from a combination of the minter's address, their choice from one of 50 seeds, and options to adjust how the generated surface is presented.

Palettes are named after native New Zealand birds and range from the subdued to the colorful.

There are no especially rare traits so mint what you love :)

---------------

Controls:
S - Export as png
H - Toggle high resolution
